attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# MHPCO Claim Office - Example Mapping
2	
3	This is the shared spec memory for Red/Green/Refactor subagents. It is the
4	single source of truth for field names, rules, examples, and per-test
5	rationale.
6	
7	## Rules
8	
9	### R1. Item base values & base premiums
10	- Sword: insurance value 1000 G, base premium 100 G
11	- Amulet: insurance value 600 G, base premium 60 G
12	- Staff: insurance value 800 G, base premium 80 G
13	- Potion: insurance value 400 G, base premium 40 G
14	- Components (rune, moonstone): insurance value 250 G each, base premium
15	  25 G per component
16	
17	### R2. Block-of-3-alike component discount
18	- Per item type, every group of exactly 3 alike components forms a block.
19	  A block costs 60 G base premium (instead of 3 × 25 = 75 G).
20	- "Alike" means **same `type`** (a rune and a moonstone are NOT alike).
21	- Blocks apply per item type independently: e.g. 3 runes + 3 moonstones =
22	  two blocks.
23	- Block requires exactly 3; 4 alike components → no block (4 × 25 = 100 G).
24	  7 alike → no block (7 × 25 = 175 G — only exact multiples wouldn't
25	  even apply because the example explicitly says "4 runes → 100 G no
26	  block; 7 runes → 175 G". Blocks are not "every 3", they are "exactly
27	  3". So 6 runes would also not be a block under this reading.)
28	  - However, the natural reading combined with the multi-block example
29	    ("3 runes + 3 moonstones → 120 G base = two separate blocks") shows
30	    a block forms only when the *whole* group is exactly 3 of the same
31	    type. With 4 runes there is no way to peel off a block of 3 and
32	    leave 1 standalone — the spec explicitly disallows this. So the
33	    rule is: a block of 3 only applies when the count is exactly 3 per
34	    type.
35	
36	### R3. Premium modifiers (per item or per policy)
37	Item-specific (apply to the *affected item's* base premium):
38	- Cursed: +50% surcharge
39	- Highly enchanted (enchantment ≥ 5): +30% surcharge
40	
41	Policy-wide (apply to the policy base premium = sum of item base premiums):
42	- Loyalty (customer has ≥ 2 years with MHPCO): −20% discount
43	- First insurance: +10% surcharge (applies to EVERY quote — each item in
44	  every quote is treated as a first insurance, regardless of customer
45	  history). Per the integration example, this is added on top of the
46	  policy base premium.
47	- Follow-up contract: −15% discount on each contract after the first
48	  (so quote step index ≥ 1 in the scenario)
49	- Processing fee: +5 G flat, added at the very end
50	
51	### R4. Rounding (in MHPCO's favor)
52	- Premiums: round **up** to whole G
53	- Payouts: round **down** to whole G
54	- Intermediate amounts are kept as fractions; only the final amount
55	  is rounded
56	
57	### R5. Claim processing - deductible & cap
58	- 100 G deductible applies **per damage event** (per entry in `damages`)
59	- Total payout per policy capped at 2 × insurance sum (the sum of items'
60	  unmodified insurance values)
61	- The cap is depleted across multiple successive claims; once exhausted,
62	  no further payout
63	
64	### R6. Damage modifiers
65	- Enchantment ≥ 8: payout = 50% of damage amount (applied BEFORE
66	  deductible)
67	- Dragon material: full reimbursement (then deductible)
68	- If both apply, the 50% rule wins (then deductible)
69	- For non-magical items (no enchantment/material like runes) only the
70	  standard rule applies
71	
72	### R7. Edge cases / errors
73	- Empty item list in a quote → premium = 5 G (just the processing fee)
74	- Quote with unknown item type → CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr,
75	  no `results` written to stdout
76	- Claim references damage to an item type not in the policy → CLI exits
77	  non-zero, error to stderr
78	- Claim with negative damage amount → CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr
79	- Damage array contains more entries of a type than insured (e.g. two
80	  sword damages but only one sword) → CLI exits non-zero, whole claim
81	  rejected
82	
83	## Interface Contract (binding field names)
84	
85	### Input (stdin JSON)
86	
87	```json
88	{
89	  "customer": {
90	    "yearsWithMHPCO": 5
91	  },
92	  "steps": [
93	    {
94	      "op": "quote",
95	      "items": [
96	        {
97	          "type": "amulet",
98	          "material": "silver",
99	          "enchantment": 2,
100	          "cursed": false
101	        }
102	      ]
103	    },
104	    {
105	      "op": "claim",
106	      "policy": 0,
107	      "incident": {
108	        "cause": "fire",
109	        "damages": [
110	          { "itemType": "amulet", "amount": 200 }
111	        ]
112	      }
113	    }
114	  ]
115	}
116	```
117	
118	Field notes:
119	- `customer.yearsWithMHPCO`: integer
120	- Quote step item fields:
121	  - `type`: one of `sword | amulet | staff | potion | rune | moonstone`
122	  - `material`: string (e.g. `"dragon"`, `"steel"`, `"silver"`)
123	  - `enchantment`: integer
124	  - `cursed`: boolean
125	  - `material`, `enchantment`, `cursed` are optional (defaults: not
126	    dragon, 0, false)
127	- Claim step: `policy` is zero-based index into `steps` referencing the
128	  earlier quote step
129	- Damage entry: `itemType` matches the `type` field used in quote items;
130	  `amount` is integer
131	
132	### Output (stdout JSON)
133	
134	```json
135	{
136	  "results": [
137	    { "premium": 11 },
138	    { "payout": 100, "remainingCap": 1100 }
139	  ]
140	}
141	```
142	
143	- `results.length === steps.length`, same order
144	- Quote result: `{ "premium": <integer> }`
145	- Claim result: `{ "payout": <integer>, "remainingCap": <integer> }`
146	
147	## Examples
148	
149	### R1 Base premiums
150	- Single sword (steel, ench 0, not cursed), customer 0 years, no prior
151	  contract → base 100 + 10 first ins + 5 fee = 115 G
152	- Single amulet → 60 + 6 + 5 = 71 G
153	- Single staff → 80 + 8 + 5 = 93 G
154	- Single potion → 40 + 4 + 5 = 49 G
155	- Single rune → 25 + 2.5 + 5 → 32.5 → round up = 33 G
156	- Single moonstone → 25 + 2.5 + 5 → 33 G
157	
158	### R2 Block of 3 alike
159	- 2 runes: 2×25 = 50 G base premium
160	- 3 runes: 60 G base premium (block applies)
161	- 4 runes: 4×25 = 100 G base premium (no block)
162	- 7 runes: 7×25 = 175 G base premium
163	- 2 runes + 1 moonstone: 2×25 + 1×25 = 75 G base premium (no block)
164	- 3 runes + 3 moonstones: 60 + 60 = 120 G base premium (two blocks)
165	
166	### R3 Modifier scope
167	- Cursed sword (base 100) + plain amulet (base 60):
168	  - policy base 160 G
169	  - cursed surcharge: 50% × 100 = +50 G (on cursed item only)
170	  - subtotal 210 G before further modifiers and fee
171	
172	### R3 Modifier thresholds
173	- yearsWithMHPCO exactly 2 → loyalty applies
174	- enchantment exactly 5 → high-enchantment applies
175	- cursed + enchantment 5 → both surcharges apply
176	- enchantment 4 → no high-enchantment surcharge
177	
178	### R5 Deductible per damage event
179	- Dragon attack damages sword (500) and amulet (300); both dragon-
180	  material? No — the example just says "dragon attack" but does not say
181	  the items are dragon material. Re-reading: "a dragon attack damages
182	  an insured sword (500 G) and an insured amulet (300 G); payout = 600 G
183	  (the 100 G deductible applies once per damaged item)" — that's
184	  (500−100) + (300−100) = 400 + 200 = 600. So neither special clause
185	  is invoked; the example is purely about deductible-per-event.
186	
187	### R5 Standard reimbursement
188	- Sword steel ench 3, damage 500 → 500 − 100 = 400 G payout
189	- Rune damage 200 G → 200 − 100 = 100 G payout
190	
191	### R6 Enchantment vs dragon
192	- Dragon sword ench 9, damage 1000 → 500 (50%) − 100 = 400 G
193	- Dragon sword ench 5, damage 800 → 800 − 100 = 700 G (only dragon
194	  clause applies; enchantment 5 < 8 so no 50% rule)
195	- Steel sword ench 9, damage 1000 → 500 − 100 = 400 G
196	- Dragon sword ench 8, damage 1000 → 500 − 100 = 400 G (50% rule
197	  triggers at exactly 8)
198	
199	### R5 Multi-item & damages
200	- Policy with two swords → insurance sum 2000, cap 4000
201	- Damages array `[{type:"sword"}, {type:"sword"}]` → each its own
202	  deductible
203	- Damages contain two sword entries but only one sword insured → reject
204	
205	### R5 Cap exhaustion
206	- Sword (1000, cap 2000); two claims of 1500 G each:
207	  - first claim → payout 1400, remainingCap 600
208	  - second claim → desired 1400, but cap 600 left → payout 600,
209	    remainingCap 0
210	- Cursed sword (insurance value 1000, premium 165 with mods) → cap 2000
211	  (mods don't raise cap)
212	- Sword + 3 runes (block) → insurance sum 1000 + 3×250 = 1750
213	  (block discount does NOT lower insurance sum)
214	- Sword + amulet → insurance sum 1600, cap 3200
215	
216	### R4 Rounding
217	- Premium 197.5 → 198 (up)
218	- Payout 350.5 → 350 (down)
219	
220	### R7 Edge cases
221	- Empty items → premium 5 G
222	- Unknown type → CLI non-zero, stderr error, no results stdout
223	- Claim refs item not in policy → CLI non-zero
224	- Negative damage amount → CLI non-zero
225	
226	### Integration: Newcomer with cursed sword
227	- customer 0 years, no prior contract
228	- cursed sword (steel, ench 3)
229	- 100 base + 50 curse + 10 first ins (10% of 100 policy base) = 160
230	  + 5 fee = 165 G
231	
232	### Integration: Long-standing customer's 2nd contract
233	- customer 3 years, 2nd quote in scenario
234	- cursed sword (steel, ench 7)
235	- 100 base + 50 curse + 30 high ench − 20 loyalty (20% of 100 base)
236	  + 10 first ins (10% of 100 base) − 15 follow-up (15% of 100 base)
237	  = 155 + 5 fee = 160 G
238	- Note: all the policy-wide percentages here happen to be on a single
239	  100 G item, so they look like flat amounts. The general rule is
240	  percentages of the policy base premium.
241	
242	## Questions / Clarifications (from ❓ in spec)
243	
244	1. **"Alike" components**: same TYPE (rune ≠ moonstone). Resolved by the
245	   3 runes + 3 moonstones = 2 blocks example.
246	2. **Modifier scope on multi-item policies**: item-specific modifiers
247	   (cursed, high enchantment) apply to that item's base premium only;
248	   policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, first insurance, follow-up) apply to
249	   the policy base premium (sum of item bases); fee added at the end.
250	3. **Multiple items of the same type**: allowed; each contributes
251	   independently to insurance sum, premium, and damage matching. If
252	   `damages` lists more of a type than insured → reject.
253	4. **First insurance vs long-standing customer**: first insurance
254	   surcharge applies to EVERY quote in EVERY scenario — each item in a
255	   quote is treated as first insurance regardless of customer history.
256	
257	## Per-Test Rationale
258	
259	Tests are split into 4 describe blocks: `quote` premium calculations,
260	`claim` payouts, edge cases / errors, and CLI integration. Within each
261	block, tests go simple → complex.
262	
263	### Quote (premium calculation)
264	
265	1. **empty items → 5 G** — R7 (only fee)
266	2. **single sword → 115 G** — R1 single item baseline; verifies base
267	   + first insurance + fee
268	3. **single amulet → 71 G** — R1 amulet base
269	4. **single staff → 93 G** — R1 staff base
270	5. **single potion → 49 G** — R1 potion base
271	6. **2 runes → no block, 33 G** — R1 + R2 (2 × 25 = 50, +10% first ins
272	   = 55, +5 fee = 60 G). Wait — let me recompute. Customer 0 yrs, first
273	   ever. 2 runes: base 50. Policy base 50. First ins +10% = 5. Subtotal
274	   55. Fee +5 = 60. So expected 60 G, not 33. (The 33 G I wrote earlier
275	   was for a single rune.) Let me correct: single rune: base 25 +
276	   first ins 2.5 + fee 5 = 32.5 → 33.
277	7. **3 runes → block, 71 G** — R2 (3 runes = 60 base; +6 first ins;
278	   +5 fee = 71)
279	8. **4 runes → no block, 115 G** — R2 (100 base + 10 + 5 = 115)
280	9. **7 runes → no block, 198 G** — R2 (175 base + 17.5 + 5 = 197.5 →
281	   198 round up). Also covers R4 rounding up.
282	10. **2 runes + 1 moonstone → 88 G** — R2 mixed types, no block (75
283	    base + 7.5 + 5 = 87.5 → 88)
284	11. **3 runes + 3 moonstones → 137 G** — R2 two separate blocks
285	    (60 + 60 = 120 base; +12 first ins; +5 fee = 137)
286	12. **cursed sword (ench 3), 0 yrs, 1st contract → 165 G** —
287	    integration newcomer example
288	13. **cursed sword (ench 5) → high-ench surcharge applies** —
289	    R3 threshold exactly 5: 100 + 50 + 30 = 180 base+item; +10 first
290	    ins; +5 fee = 195 G (customer 0 yrs, no prior). Actually the
291	    surcharges are on the item base premium so policy base is still
292	    100; item surcharges add to that. So total = 100 + 50 + 30 + 10
293	    + 5 = 195.
294	14. **sword ench 4, not cursed → no surcharges, 115 G** — R3 threshold
295	15. **sword ench 5, not cursed → high-ench applies, 30 + 100 + 10 +
296	    5 = 145 G** — R3 threshold exactly 5
297	16. **customer with exactly 2 yrs → loyalty applies** — R3 threshold.
298	    Plain sword: 100 base − 20 loyalty + 10 first ins + 5 fee = 95 G
299	17. **cursed sword + plain amulet → 210 G before fee+other mods** —
300	    R3 modifier scope. Customer 0 yrs, 1st contract. Bases: 100 + 60
301	    = 160. Curse: +50 (on sword only). First ins +10% × 160 = 16.
302	    Fee +5. Total = 160 + 50 + 16 + 5 = 231 G.
303	18. **long-standing customer 2nd contract: cursed sword ench 7 →
304	    160 G** — integration follow-up example
305	19. **rounding: premium yielding 197.5 → 198** — R4 (covered by test 9)
306	
307	### Claim (payout calculation)
308	
309	20. **steel sword ench 3, damage 500 → payout 400, remainingCap 1600**
310	    — R5 standard reimbursement; cap was 2000, used 400, 1600 left
311	21. **rune damage 200 → payout 100, remainingCap 400** — R5 standard
312	    rune (insurance value 250, cap 500, used 100, 400 left)
313	22. **dragon sword ench 5, damage 800 → payout 700, remainingCap
314	    1300** — R6 dragon-only clause (full reimburse, then deductible)
315	23. **steel sword ench 9, damage 1000 → payout 400, remainingCap 1600**
316	    — R6 high-ench only (50% first, then deductible)
317	24. **dragon sword ench 9, damage 1000 → payout 400, remainingCap
318	    1600** — R6 both clauses; 50% rule wins
319	25. **dragon sword ench 8, damage 1000 → payout 400, remainingCap
320	    1600** — R6 threshold exactly 8
321	26. **deductible per damage event: damages [sword 500, amulet 300] on
322	    sword+amulet policy → payout 600, remainingCap 2600** — R5
323	    deductible per event. Cap = 3200, used 600, 2600 left.
324	27. **two swords insured, damages [sword 500, sword 300] → payout
325	    600, remainingCap 3400** — multi-item same type. Cap 4000, used
326	    600.
327	28. **cap exhaustion: sword, two claims of 1500 each → first
328	    payout 1400 remaining 600; second payout 600 remaining 0** —
329	    R5 cap exhaustion (2 separate claim steps in one scenario)
330	29. **rounding: payout 350.5 → 350** — R4 rounding down. Use dragon
331	    sword ench 9, damage 801 → 400.5 − 100 = 300.5 → 300? Actually
332	    50% of 801 = 400.5, then −100 deductible = 300.5 → rounded down
333	    to 300. So pick a damage that yields .5 fractional payout. Use
334	    damage 901: 50% of 901 = 450.5, −100 = 350.5 → 350. Good.
335	
336	### Edge cases / errors
337	
338	30. **empty items → premium 5 G** — already covered as quote test 1
339	31. **unknown item type in quote → CLI exits non-zero, stderr
340	    contains error, stdout has no results** — R7
341	32. **claim references item type not in policy → CLI exits non-zero,
342	    stderr error** — R7
343	33. **negative damage amount → CLI exits non-zero, stderr error** — R7
344	34. **damages contain more entries of a type than insured → CLI exits
345	    non-zero, whole claim rejected** — R7 multi-item edge case
346	
347	### CLI integration (stdin/stdout)
348	
349	35. **CLI reads JSON from stdin, writes results JSON to stdout (basic
350	    quote scenario)** — the schema example: customer 5 yrs, amulet
351	    quote then amulet claim
352	36. **CLI processes multi-step scenario: quote then claim returns
353	    `{ results: [{ premium }, { payout, remainingCap }] }`** — same
354	    as 35 with both step results validated
355	37. **CLI exits with non-zero code on unknown item type** — covered by
356	    31; verify via CLI invocation
357	38. **CLI exits with non-zero code on invalid claim** — covered by 32
358	39. **CLI exits with non-zero code on negative damage amount** — by 33
359

content.text:
Now I have full context. The next test to activate is test #22: "policy with dragon-material sword (enchantment 5); damage 800 → payout 700 G (only dragon clause: full reimbursement, then deductible), remainingCap 1300".
